title: Leftists expect the world to ‘sit politely’ as Iran funds terror
description: Sky News host Sharri Markson says critics on the left have called US President Donald Trump’s Iranian strikes “illegal aggression”.  Ms Markson said people on the left expect the world to “sit politely” while the Iranian regime funds terror.  “And threatens everyone around it.”

title: ‘Pure evil on Earth’: ISIS brides follow an ideology ‘trained in martyrdom’
description: Retired Special Forces Major Heston Russell recounts his experience fighting ISIS as Australia juggles with the repatriation of ISIS brides.    “These are ideologically addicted people, they are fanatical, they have no care for life, I’ve been exposed to a lot of death and destruction in my time, these people trade in it on the daily,” Mr Russell told Sky News host Sharri Markson.  “You don’t walk away from that ideology … these people are trained in martyrdom, that death and doing this destruction will bring them salvation and profit in the afterlife.”

title: ‘Fanatical’: Australian public must come first as ISIS brides spark security concerns
description: Retired Special Forces Major Heston Russell details the security concerns over the ISIS brides.   “I don’t understand how any Australian citizen going to a known war zone and interacting with, let alone living with, let alone taking minors, children to be associated with known terrorist individuals and organisations is not a crime that sees them incarcerated as soon as they come to Australia,” Mr Russell told Sky News host Sharri Markson.  “This ideology is so fanatical, so brutal, so evil, that any perceived risk to the Australian public has to come first.”

title: ‘More effective, cheaper, harder to stop’: Iran’s drone strategy reshaping Middle East conflict
description: Strategic Analysis Australia Director Michael Shoebridge discusses Iran’s utilisation of drone warfare.   “The drone is more effective, it’s cheaper, it’s easier to conceal, and you don’t need the big launcher that missiles do,” Mr Shoebridge told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “We’ll see more drone attacks in the coming days and weeks.  “It’s like a flying bomb.”

title: Albanese accused of ‘turning his back’ on Australian values after Muslim funding scandal
description: Shadow Attorney-General Michaelia Cash says it is “appalling” that Prime Minister Albanese “turned his back on Australian values” by funding a Muslim community organisation mourning the passing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.  “This is completely, totally and utterly unacceptable behaviour, mourning the death of the person who led a listed terrorist organisation in Australia,” Ms Cash told Sky News host Sharri Markson.   “Mr Albanese has yet again shown the Australian people he doesn’t have the backbone, the spine or the guts to look them in the eye and say what has occurred in these organisations … it is completely un-Australian.”

title: ‘Totally unacceptable’: Albanese under fire for funding groups mourning ‘brutal dictator’
description: Shadow Attorney-General, Michaelia Cash says it is “un-Australian” and “unacceptable” for the Albanese government to use taxpayer funds to grant an Islamic organisation that mourned the death of “one of the most murderous people in the world”.   Ms Cash told Sky News host Sharri Markson that Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was a “brutal dictator” who “exported terrorism overseas”.   “Mr Albanese needs to announce tomorrow … I am now announcing an audit of any organisation that mourned the death of the murderous dictator.   “Australian taxpayers hard earned dollar should not to go these organisations.”

title: ‘Declaration of war’ between US and Iran has not been made
description: UK Lawyers for Israel Legal Director Natasha Hausdorff says there still has not been a “declaration of war” between the US and Iran.  Ms Hausdorff said it’s an “attack” on the US Constitution to suggest Donald Trump’s strikes in Iran violate the legal framework.  “This is not an accurate portrayal of what the law says.”

title: ‘Don’t believe a word Albanese is saying’: PM accused of ‘misleading’ the public on ISIS cohort
description: Shadow Attorney-General Michaelia Cash says the Albanese government is “misleading” the Australian people in relation to the ISIS cohort returning to Australia.   “It is lying to the Australian people every step of the way in relation to the assistance that is given to the ISIS brides,” Ms Cash told Sky News host Sharri Markson.   “What more proof could you need?  “This government throws its hands up and uses every bit of weasel words in the book to make excuses.”

title: ‘In the firing line’: Iranian drone strikes Australian facilities in Dubai
description: Sky News host Paul Murray details how an Iranian drone strike hit Australian facilities in Dubai.   “There was an Iranian drone strike which apparently had hit Australian facilities in and around Dubai,” Mr Murray said.  “They are in the firing line.”

title: ‘Absolute loser of a PM’: Farage hits out at Starmer for being ‘terrified’ of speaking on Iran
description: Reform UK Leader Nigel Farage calls UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer an “absolute disgrace” for his underwhelming reaction to what is happening in Iran.   “I do believe that morally what Trump is trying to do, what Israel is trying to do, is right,” Mr Farage told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “The French have stepped up and supported the Americans, the Australian government has said supportive things, the Canadian government has said supportive things.   “And what have we got? We’ve got an absolute loser of a prime minister who follows, doesn’t lead.  “He’s terrified of alienating [Muslim] vote.”

title: Nigel Farage shames Labour for refusing to list IRGC as a terrorist organisation
description: Reform UK Leader Nigel Farage thinks it is ridiculous the UK Labour government cannot list the IRGC as a terrorist organisation.   This comes after there were “pro-Iranian regime demonstrations on the streets of London,” Mr Farage told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “Yet British governments won’t do it.”

title: ‘The democratic system needs fundamental reform’: Nigel Farage fed up with immigrant voters
description: Reform UK Leader Nigel Farage claims “mass migration” is to blame for his party coming second in the recent by-election.   “There were thousands of people with Pakistani passports who, just because they can show they have a settled address in the UK, were voting last Thursday,” Mr Farage told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “The whole democratic system needs fundamental reform.  “I’m going to fight for the integrity of our democratic system … I will win.”

title: ‘No way’ Iran conflict becomes long war, Vance declares
description: Sky News host Freya Leach reacts to US Vice President JD Vance’s recent comments regarding the war in Iran.

title: ‘Good on them’: Iranian women’s soccer team boycotts national anthem amid US-Iran conflict
description: Sky news host Caleb Bond details how the Iranian women's soccer team decided to boycott their own anthem amid the US-Iran conflict.

title: ‘Fair or not fair’: AI cameras used on roads scrutinised
description: Sky News host Freya Leach questions whether it is fair or “unfair” for people to get fined due to incorrect driving behaviour on AI cameras.   “AI cameras are being used to photograph people in their cars to determine whether they are using their phone or wearing their seatbelts incorrectly,” Ms Leach said.   “One 85-year-old mother said she briefly pulled her seatbelt away from her to turn around and speak to children who were in the backseat.  “The camera caught her at exactly that moment, and she ended up with a $555 fine, and it was double demerit points because it was over the Christmas period.   “That is probably one per cent of the cases where there is a genuinely unfair reason you’ve been caught out.”
